title: Error when trying podman inspect --format
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I get an error when I try to inspect Network Ports. I can get a list but not individual elements
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start with podman run a stopped container pw2

2.  podman inspect --format='{{range $p, $conf := .NetworkSettings.Ports}} {{$p}} -> {{(index $conf 0).HostPort}} {{end}}' pw2
3.

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: Template parsing error: template: image:1:59: executing "image" at <index $conf 0>: error calling index: can't index item of type ocicni.PortMapping

**Describe the results you expected:**
I should get 3000 and 8080
podman inspect --format='{{.NetworkSettings.Ports}}' pw2
[{3000 3000 tcp } {8080 8080 tcp }]

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This is part of why I really dislike templating. It requires that our internal data representations match Docker, which is not a promise that I want to make.

In this case, the format for our port structures in `podman inspect` looks to differ from the format Docker uses. We can probably reformat our output to match Docker more closely. I'll try and find some time to look into it, but this is fairly low on my priorities list. Other contributors are welcome to take this one given that.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Podman has `HostConfig.PortBindings` can match docker for the port configs, but doesn't support field `.NetworkSettings.Ports` like docker does. Do we plan to change the Ports filed under InspectNetworkSettings to a map type?
```
$ docker  inspect --format='{{range $p, $conf := .HostConfig.PortBindings}} {{$p}} -> {{(index $conf 0).HostPort}} {{end}}' ctr
 8080/tcp -> 8000 
$ podman  inspect --format='{{range $p, $conf := .HostConfig.PortBindings}} {{$p}} -> {{(index $conf 0).HostPort}} {{end}}' ctr
   8080/tcp   ->   8080
```

We have no intention of changing database storage format for ports, but we
could do the conversion to Docker format as part of inspect (could be slow,
though, pretty ranges can easily make thousands of port structures)

On Fri, Jun 19, 2020, 11:28 Qi Wang <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Podman has HostConfig.PortBindings can match docker for the port configs,
> but doesn't support field .NetworkSettings.Ports like docker does. Do we
> plan to change the Ports filed under InspectNetworkSettings to a map type?
>
> $ docker  inspect --format='{{range $p, $conf := .HostConfig.PortBindings}} {{$p}} -> {{(index $conf 0).HostPort}} {{end}}' ctr
>  8080/tcp -> 8000
> $ podman  inspect --format='{{range $p, $conf := .HostConfig.PortBindings}} {{$p}} -> {{(index $conf 0).HostPort}} {{end}}' ctr
>    8080/tcp   ->   8080
>
